keyEvents.event: Defeat in World War II and atomic bombings
keyEvents.impact: Constitutional pacifism (Article 9) and unique nuclear allergy as the only nation attacked with atomic weapons
Security outsourced to the American alliance; focus shifted entirely to economic recovery
Economic miracle validated the Yoshida Doctrine of trade over arms

keyEvents.event: Asset bubble collapse and Lost Decades
keyEvents.impact: Prolonged stagnation, deflation, and demographic decline constrain ambition
Energy insecurity deepened; reliance on imported fuel and sea lanes underscored
Gradual normalization of the military role within the US alliance

keyEvents.event: New National Security Strategy doubles defense spending
keyEvents.impact: Counterstrike capability and 2% GDP defense budget in response to China and North Korea

history.narrative: A pacifist trading state slowly shedding postwar restraints as China's rise and North Korean missiles erode its sense of safety. Acts almost exclusively through the US alliance and economic statecraft, with deep reluctance toward any use of force but a first-class latent military-industrial base.

redLines.description: Chinese seizure of the Senkaku Islands or attack on Japanese territory
North Korean missile strike or nuclear threat against Japan
Collapse of the US security guarantee in the Pacific
Closure of sea lanes carrying energy and food imports

internalDivisions.name: Pacifists vs constitutional revisionists over Article 9
Okinawan opposition to US base burden

goals.description: Deter Chinese expansion in the East China Sea and a Taiwan contingency
Defend against North Korean missile and nuclear threats
Keep the US committed to Indo-Pacific security
Secure energy supplies and semiconductor supply chains
Shape regional order through the Quad and economic diplomacy
